_comment: Curated CNS evidence scores for drugs with documented neurological efficacy. Score reflects strength of clinical/preclinical CNS penetration evidence. Source: FDA labels, published PK studies, preclinical efficacy data.

everolimus.evidence: FDA-approved for TSC-associated SEGA and partial-onset seizures (CNS indications). CSF penetration documented. Reference: Franz et al., 2013.

sirolimus.evidence: Preclinical CNS efficacy in SYNGAP1, TSC, and NF1 mouse models. CNS exposure sufficient for mTOR inhibition at systemic doses. Reference: Bhatt et al., 2020.

temsirolimus.evidence: CNS penetration documented in neuro-oncology studies. mTOR inhibitor class effect.

selumetinib.evidence: FDA-approved for NF1 plexiform neurofibromas (pediatric >= 2 years). Active trials for NF1 optic pathway gliomas confirm CNS penetration. Reference: Dombi et al., 2016.

trametinib.evidence: CNS penetration documented in pediatric low-grade glioma trials. MEK inhibitor class with CNS activity. Reference: Fangusaro et al., 2019.

binimetinib.evidence: CNS activity in melanoma brain metastasis trials. MEK inhibitor.

cobimetinib.evidence: Some CNS activity data from melanoma studies.

metformin.evidence: Crosses BBB via OCT transporters. CNS effects documented in multiple neurological conditions. Reference: Ou et al., 2018.

lovastatin.evidence: Lipophilic statin with documented CNS penetration. Cognitive effects studied in NF1. Reference: Krab et al., 2008.

simvastatin.evidence: Lipophilic statin, high CNS penetration vs. hydrophilic statins. Neuroprotective effects documented.

vigabatrin.evidence: FDA-approved antiepileptic with primary CNS target. Direct GABA mechanism.

valproic acid.evidence: Classic CNS drug. FDA-approved for epilepsy. High CNS penetration.

memantine.evidence: FDA-approved for Alzheimer's. Primary CNS target (NMDA receptor).

ketamine.evidence: Anesthetic/CNS drug with primary CNS target.

lithium.evidence: Primary CNS drug (mood stabilizer). Well-documented CNS penetration.

minocycline.evidence: Crosses BBB. Studied in multiple CNS conditions including Fragile X. Reference: Bhatt et al.

rapamycin.evidence: Same as sirolimus (rapamycin is the INN for sirolimus). CNS efficacy in multiple mouse models.

cabozantinib.evidence: Some CNS penetration data from GBM trials.

erlotinib.evidence: Limited CNS penetration. Some data in EGFR-mutant CNS metastases.
